title: phalacroloma annuum subsp. strigosum
intro: Phalacroloma annuum subsp. strigosum, also known as the Desert Marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phalacroloma annuum subsp. strigosum include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and flowers are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed for their medicinal properties.
